books.title: Paradise Lost
books.author: John Milton
books.summary: The epic poem of humanity's fall from grace, featuring Satan's rebellion against God.
books.historical_context: Written during the English Restoration, reflecting religious and political tensions of the time.

books.title: The Book of the Dead
books.author: Anonymous
books.summary: Collection of spells and instructions for the journey through the afterlife.
books.historical_context: Developed over centuries in ancient Egypt, fundamental to Egyptian religious beliefs.

books.title: The Divine Comedy
books.author: Dante Alighieri
books.summary: Allegorical journey through the afterlife realms of Hell, Purgatory, and Paradise.
books.historical_context: Written during Dante's exile from Florence, reflecting medieval Christian theology and Italian politics.

books.title: The Canterbury Tales
books.author: Geoffrey Chaucer
books.summary: A collection of stories told by pilgrims traveling to Canterbury Cathedral, providing a vivid portrait of medieval English society.
books.historical_context: Written during the late Middle Ages, depicting the social dynamics and cultural values of medieval England across different social classes.

books.title: The Iliad
books.author: Homer
books.summary: The epic tale of the Trojan War, focusing on the wrath of Achilles and its consequences.
books.historical_context: Composed in the 8th century BCE, reflecting Bronze Age warfare and Greek mythology.

books.title: The Odyssey
books.author: Homer
books.summary: Odysseus's ten-year journey home after the Trojan War, facing mythical creatures and divine intervention.
books.historical_context: Composed alongside The Iliad, depicting Greek maritime culture and the importance of hospitality in ancient society.

books.title: The Aeneid
books.author: Virgil
books.summary: The journey of Aeneas from Troy to Italy, where he becomes the ancestor of the Romans.
books.historical_context: Written during Augustus's reign, establishing a mythical connection between Troy and Rome.

books.title: Gulliver's Travels
books.author: Jonathan Swift
books.summary: Lemuel Gulliver's voyages to various strange lands, satirizing human nature and society.
books.historical_context: Written as a satire of travel narratives and contemporary European politics.

books.title: The Decameron
books.author: Giovanni Boccaccio
books.summary: A collection of 100 tales told by ten young people sheltering from the Black Death.
books.historical_context: Written during the aftermath of the Black Death, reflecting medieval Italian society and values.

books.title: The Tale of the Heike
books.author: Anonymous
books.summary: The epic account of the struggle between the Taira and Minamoto clans for control of Japan.
books.historical_context: Chronicles the Genpei War (1180-1185), marking the end of the classical period in Japanese history.

books.title: Dead Souls
books.author: Nikolai Gogol
books.summary: Chichikov's scheme to buy deceased serfs' names from landowners, revealing the absurdities of Russian society.
books.historical_context: Written during the reform period of Nicholas I, satirizing Russian serfdom and bureaucracy.

books.title: The Master and Margarita
books.author: Mikhail Bulgakov
books.summary: Satan visits Soviet Moscow, interweaving a love story with a satirical critique of Soviet society.
books.historical_context: Written during Stalin's regime, published posthumously, reflecting the absurdities of Soviet life.

books.title: The Man Without Qualities
books.author: Robert Musil
books.summary: Ulrich's search for meaning in the last days of the Austro-Hungarian Empire.
books.historical_context: Set before World War I, examining the decline of the Habsburg Empire and modern alienation.

books.title: The Life and Opinions of Tristram Shandy
books.author: Laurence Sterne
books.summary: The rambling, digressive autobiography of Tristram Shandy, who cannot even manage to get himself born.
books.historical_context: Written during the Age of Enlightenment, experimenting with narrative conventions.

books.title: Fathers and Sons
books.author: Ivan Turgenev
books.summary: The conflict between generations and ideologies in 1850s Russia through the story of nihilist Bazarov.
books.historical_context: Written during the period of great reforms in Russia, examining the generational divide.

books.title: The Tin Drum
books.author: Günter Grass
books.summary: Oskar Matzerath, who decides not to grow up, witnesses the rise and fall of Nazi Germany.
books.historical_context: Written in post-war Germany, examining the Nazi period through magical realism.

books.title: The Recognition of Śakuntalā
books.author: Kālidāsa
books.summary: The love story of King Dushyanta and Shakuntala, separated by a curse and reunited through a ring.
books.historical_context: Written during India's classical period, exemplifying Sanskrit dramatic traditions.

books.title: The Dream of the Red Chamber
books.author: Cao Xueqin
books.summary: The rise and fall of the Jia family and the love story between Jia Baoyu and Lin Daiyu.
books.historical_context: Written during the Qing Dynasty, depicting the decline of noble families.

books.title: Season of Migration to the North
books.author: Tayeb Salih
books.summary: A Sudanese man's experiences in England and his return home, exploring colonialism and identity.
books.historical_context: Written post-independence, examining the complex relationship between Africa and Europe.

books.title: The Golden Notebook
books.author: Doris Lessing
books.summary: Writer Anna Wulf's attempt to integrate different aspects of her life through four notebooks.
books.historical_context: Written during the Cold War, exploring feminism, communism, and mental breakdown.

books.title: Death Comes for the Archbishop
books.author: Willa Cather
books.summary: The life of a French Catholic priest building a diocese in the American Southwest.
books.historical_context: Set in the mid-19th century, depicting the American frontier and cultural encounters.

books.title: The Plague
books.author: Albert Camus
books.summary: A city's response to a deadly plague, examining human resilience and solidarity.
books.historical_context: Written after World War II, using plague as an allegory for the Nazi occupation.

books.title: The Master of Go
books.author: Yasunari Kawabata
books.summary: A championship Go match between an old master and a modern challenger.
books.historical_context: Based on a real 1938 match, symbolizing the clash between tradition and modernity in Japan.

books.title: Austerlitz
books.author: W.G. Sebald
books.summary: An architectural historian discovers his Jewish origins and childhood escape from Nazi Germany.
books.historical_context: Written at the turn of the millennium, examining Holocaust memory and European history.

books.title: The Leopard
books.author: Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa
books.summary: A Sicilian aristocrat witnesses the decline of his class during Italian unification.
books.historical_context: Set during the Risorgimento, examining social change in 19th century Italy.

books.title: The Tale of Kieu
books.author: Nguyễn Du
books.summary: A virtuous woman's sacrifices and suffering to save her family, written in verse.
books.historical_context: Written during the Nguyen dynasty, reflecting Vietnamese cultural values and Chinese influence.

books.title: Pedro Páramo
books.author: Juan Rulfo
books.summary: A man's search for his father in a ghost town populated by spirits of the dead.
books.historical_context: Written after the Mexican Revolution, examining power, death, and Mexican identity.

books.title: The Complete Stories of Flannery O'Connor
books.author: Flannery O'Connor
books.summary: Collection of stories exploring faith, morality, and the grotesque in the American South.
books.historical_context: Written during the Civil Rights era, examining religious faith and racial tensions.

books.title: The Book of Disquiet
books.author: Fernando Pessoa
books.summary: The fragmentary thoughts and observations of Bernardo Soares, a bookkeeper in Lisbon.
books.historical_context: Written during Portugal's transition from monarchy to dictatorship, exploring modern alienation.

books.title: Blood Meridian
books.author: Cormac McCarthy
books.summary: A violent journey through the American-Mexican borderlands in the 1850s.
books.historical_context: Based on historical events, examining the violence of American westward expansion.

books.title: The Mahabharata
books.author: Vyasa
books.summary: Epic tale of the Kurukshetra War and its aftermath, exploring dharma and moral duty.
books.historical_context: Composed over centuries, fundamental to Hindu philosophy and Indian culture.

books.title: The Possessed
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.summary: A group of revolutionaries bring chaos to a small Russian town.
books.historical_context: Written in response to real revolutionary movements in Russia, examining nihilism and political radicalism.

books.title: The Makioka Sisters
books.author: Junichiro Tanizaki
books.summary: The decline of an aristocratic Osaka family through the lives of four sisters.
books.historical_context: Set before World War II, depicting the conflict between tradition and modernity in Japan.

books.title: If on a winter's night a traveler
books.author: Italo Calvino
books.summary: A novel about reading novels, where the reader becomes a character in the story.
books.historical_context: Written during postmodernism's height, experimenting with narrative structure and reader involvement.

books.title: The Castle
books.author: Franz Kafka
books.summary: K.'s futile attempts to gain access to the mysterious authorities in the Castle.
books.historical_context: Written during the dissolution of the Austro-Hungarian Empire, examining bureaucracy and alienation.

books.title: Death in Venice
books.author: Thomas Mann
books.summary: An aging writer becomes obsessed with a young boy while visiting Venice during a cholera epidemic.
books.historical_context: Written before World War I, examining art, beauty, and decay in European culture.

books.title: Journey to the West
books.author: Wu Cheng'en
books.summary: The magical journey of a monk and his supernatural companions to retrieve Buddhist scriptures.
books.historical_context: Written during the Ming Dynasty, based on the historical journey of Xuanzang.

books.title: The Stories of Jorge Luis Borges
books.author: Jorge Luis Borges
books.summary: Collection of metaphysical stories exploring infinity, time, and identity.
books.historical_context: Written during Argentina's transition from traditional to modern society, influencing magical realism.

books.title: The Ramayana
books.author: Valmiki
books.summary: The epic journey of Rama to rescue his wife Sita from the demon king Ravana.
books.historical_context: Ancient Sanskrit epic that has profoundly influenced Indian culture and literature.

books.title: The Flowers of Evil
books.author: Charles Baudelaire
books.summary: Poetry collection exploring urban life, modernity, and the nature of evil and beauty.
books.historical_context: Written during Paris's modernization, marking the beginning of modern poetry.

books.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
books.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.summary: Collection of detective stories featuring the brilliant consulting detective Sherlock Holmes.
books.historical_context: Written during Victorian England's scientific advancement and urbanization.

books.title: The Alexandria Quartet
books.author: Lawrence Durrell
books.summary: Four interconnected novels exploring love and modern life in pre-war Alexandria.
books.historical_context: Set before and during World War II, examining colonialism and cultural intersection.

books.title: The Waves
books.author: Virginia Woolf
books.summary: The intertwined lives of six friends told through interior monologues.
books.historical_context: Written during the modernist period, experimenting with stream of consciousness.

books.title: Snow Country
books.author: Yasunari Kawabata
books.summary: The love affair between a Tokyo dilettante and a provincial geisha in the snow country.
books.historical_context: Written during Japan's modernization, examining traditional Japanese aesthetics.

books.title: The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter
books.author: Anonymous
books.summary: The story of a mysterious girl found in a bamboo stalk who turns out to be from the Moon.
books.historical_context: Oldest extant Japanese prose narrative, reflecting Heian period court culture.

books.title: Blindness
books.author: José Saramago
books.summary: A city's descent into chaos when its population is struck by an epidemic of blindness.
books.historical_context: Written at the end of the 20th century, examining human nature and social breakdown.

books.title: The Unbearable Lightness of Being
books.author: Milan Kundera
books.summary: The lives of four interconnected characters during the Prague Spring and its aftermath.
books.historical_context: Set during the Soviet invasion of Czechoslovakia, examining politics and personal freedom.

books.title: The Good Soldier
books.author: Ford Madox Ford
books.summary: The tragic relationships between two wealthy couples, told through unreliable narration.
books.historical_context: Written on the eve of World War I, examining the decay of European society.

books.title: A House for Mr. Biswas
books.author: V.S. Naipaul
books.summary: One man's lifelong struggle for independence and identity in colonial Trinidad.
books.historical_context: Set in colonial Trinidad, examining post-colonial identity and social mobility.

books.title: The Life of Samuel Johnson
books.author: James Boswell
books.summary: The definitive biography of the great literary figure Samuel Johnson.
books.historical_context: Written during the Age of Enlightenment, pioneering modern biography.

books.title: The Tale of Lady Ochikubo
books.author: Anonymous
books.summary: A Cinderella-like story of a mistreated noblewoman who rises to happiness through her talents.
books.historical_context: Written during the Heian period, depicting court life and women's experiences.

books.title: Berlin Alexanderplatz
books.author: Alfred Döblin
books.summary: An ex-convict's struggle to stay straight in the criminal underworld of 1920s Berlin.
books.historical_context: Set during the Weimar Republic, depicting urban modernism and social upheaval.

books.title: The Man of Property
books.author: John Galsworthy
books.summary: The story of the wealthy Forsyte family, examining the power of property and possession.
books.historical_context: Written during the Edwardian era, examining Victorian values and social change.

books.title: The Bridge on the Drina
books.author: Ivo Andrić
books.summary: Chronicles four centuries of Balkan history through the story of a bridge.
books.historical_context: Spanning Ottoman to Austro-Hungarian rule, examining Balkan cultural intersections.

books.title: The Great Gatsby
books.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
books.summary: A mysterious millionaire's obsessive quest for his lost love amid the decadence of the Jazz Age.
books.historical_context: Written during the Roaring Twenties, examining the American Dream and social excess.

books.title: Moby-Dick
books.author: Herman Melville
books.summary: Captain Ahab's obsessive quest for revenge against the white whale Moby Dick.
books.historical_context: Written during America's whaling era, exploring themes of obsession and nature.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.summary: Elizabeth Bennet's journey of love and self-discovery in Georgian era England.
books.historical_context: Written during the Regency period, examining class, marriage, and social expectations.

books.title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
books.author: Gabriel García Márquez
books.summary: The multi-generational saga of the Buendía family in the magical town of Macondo.
books.historical_context: Written during Latin America's Boom period, incorporating magical realism to explore Colombian history.

books.title: The Tale of Genji
books.author: Murasaki Shikibu
books.summary: The life and romantic pursuits of Hikaru Genji, the emperor's son, in the imperial court.
books.historical_context: Written during the height of the Heian period, depicting aristocratic culture and customs.

title.title: The Thousand and One Nights
title.author: Various Authors
title.summary: A collection of Middle Eastern folk tales framed by Scheherazade's nightly storytelling.
title.historical_context: Compiled during the Islamic Golden Age, reflecting medieval Islamic culture.

title.title: The Epic of Gilgamesh
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: The adventures of King Gilgamesh and his friend Enkidu, exploring themes of friendship and mortality.
title.historical_context: One of the earliest surviving works of literature, from ancient Sumerian civilization.

title.title: Beloved
title.author: Toni Morrison
title.summary: A former slave is haunted by the ghost of her daughter in post-Civil War Ohio.
title.historical_context: Set in the aftermath of the Civil War, examining the psychological impact of slavery.

title.title: The God of Small Things
title.author: Arundhati Roy
title.summary: The story of twins whose lives are destroyed by the 'Love Laws' of their community.
title.historical_context: Set in 1969, exploring post-colonial Indian society and social hierarchies.

title.title: The Sound and the Fury
title.author: William Faulkner
title.summary: The decline of the Compson family told through multiple perspectives.
title.historical_context: Set in the American South, examining the decay of Southern aristocracy.

title.title: The Stranger
title.author: Albert Camus
title.summary: Meursault's emotional detachment leads to murder and a philosophical examination of existence.
title.historical_context: Written during French colonial rule in Algeria, exploring existentialism.

title.title: The Metamorphosis
title.author: Franz Kafka
title.summary: Gregor Samsa wakes up one morning transformed into a giant insect.
title.historical_context: Written during the decline of the Austro-Hungarian Empire, exploring alienation.

title.title: Things Fall Apart
title.author: Chinua Achebe
title.summary: The life of Okonkwo and his village's encounter with British colonialism.
title.historical_context: Set in pre-colonial and colonial Nigeria, examining cultural clash.

title.title: Beowulf
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: An Old English epic poem featuring the hero Beowulf, who battles the monster Grendel, Grendel's mother, and a dragon.
title.historical_context: Likely composed between the 8th and 11th centuries, it is one of the most important works of Anglo-Saxon literature.

title.title: Shahnameh
title.author: Ferdowsi
title.summary: Epic poem narrating the legendary and historical past of Persia, from the creation of the world until the Arab conquest.
title.historical_context: Composed over 30 years, preserving Persian cultural identity after the Islamic conquest.

title.title: Song of Roland
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: An epic poem about the Battle of Roncevaux Pass in 778, focusing on the hero Roland and themes of fealty and honor.
title.historical_context: One of the earliest and most famous chansons de geste, reflecting medieval knightly values and the Carolingian legend.

title.title: Sundiata
title.author: Anonymous (oral tradition)
title.summary: The epic of Sundiata Keita, the founder of the Mali Empire, as recounted by West African griots.
title.historical_context: Passed down orally, reflecting West African cultural traditions and the empire's founding myths.

title.title: Popol Vuh
title.author: Anonymous (K'iche' Maya)
title.summary: The creation myth and epic narratives of the K'iche' Maya people, describing the origins of the world and human beings.
title.historical_context: Originally an oral tradition, transcribed in the mid-16th century following Spanish colonization.

title.title: Analects
title.author: Confucius (compiled by disciples)
title.summary: A collection of sayings and ideas attributed to Confucius and his disciples, focusing on morality, society, and governance.
title.historical_context: Central text of Confucianism, compiled during the Warring States period, profoundly influencing East Asian ethical systems.

title.title: Daodejing
title.author: Laozi
title.summary: A foundational Daoist text of 81 chapters, discussing the Dao (the Way), virtue, and natural harmony.
title.historical_context: Traditionally attributed to Laozi in the 6th century BCE, deeply shaping Chinese philosophy and religion.

title.title: Zhuangzi
title.author: Zhuang Zhou
title.summary: Philosophical work exploring the Dao, questioning conventional norms, and emphasizing spontaneity and freedom.
title.historical_context: One of the foundational Daoist texts, compiled during the Warring States period, notable for its parables and imaginative style.

title.title: Arthashastra
title.author: Kautilya (Chanakya)
title.summary: Treatise on statecraft, economic policy, and military strategy in ancient India.
title.historical_context: Attributed to Chanakya, advisor to Emperor Chandragupta Maurya, shaping governance in the Mauryan Empire.

title.title: Histories
title.author: Herodotus
title.summary: An inquiry into the origins and events of the Greco-Persian Wars, widely regarded as the first major work of history in Western literature.
title.historical_context: Written in the 5th century BCE, providing a detailed account of various peoples and cultures, pioneering historical narrative.

title.title: The Legend of Etana
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: A Mesopotamian myth recounting the adventures of Etana, a legendary king seeking the plant of birth.
title.historical_context: Attributed to early Sumerian traditions, representing heroic myth from the 19th century BCE.

title.title: Enmerkar and the Lord of Aratta
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: An epic tale of rivalry and divine mandate between the Sumerian ruler Enmerkar and the distant kingdom of Aratta.
title.historical_context: A foundational work of Sumerian literature from the 18th century BCE.

title.title: The Epic of Atrahasis
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: A Babylonian epic that recounts the creation of humanity and a great flood, prefiguring later flood myths.
title.historical_context: An influential myth from the 17th century BCE that has shaped later literary traditions.

title.title: The Tale of Two Brothers
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: A narrative of betrayal, love, and redemption between two brothers in ancient Egypt.
title.historical_context: An important piece of Egyptian literature dating from the 15th century BCE.

title.title: The Instruction of Shuruppak
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: A set of proverbial instructions attributed to the wise man Shuruppak.
title.historical_context: An early example of Sumerian wisdom literature from the 14th century BCE.

title.title: The Epos of Lugalbanda
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: An epic recounting the adventures of the legendary Sumerian king Lugalbanda.
title.historical_context: A mythic narrative from the 13th century BCE that influenced later epic traditions.

title.title: The Debate between Winter and Summer
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: A Hittite text in which personifications of winter and summer debate the merits of their seasons.
title.historical_context: A unique example of seasonal allegory from the 12th century BCE.

title.title: The Tale of Wenamun
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: A narrative detailing the adventures of Wenamun on his quest for trade and survival.
title.historical_context: An important literary work from the 11th century BCE providing insight into Egyptian society.

title.title: The Tale of the Shipwrecked Sailor
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: A story of a sailor’s miraculous survival after being shipwrecked and his encounter with a divine serpent.
title.historical_context: An enduring narrative from the 10th century BCE of ancient Egyptian maritime lore.

title.title: The Prophecies of Neferti
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: A collection of prophetic verses attributed to the wise Neferti.
title.historical_context: A work from the 9th century BCE reflecting Egyptian concerns with fate and divine order.

title.title: The Poem of the Righteous Sufferer
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: An Akkadian poem that reflects on suffering and justice in human life.
title.historical_context: A poetic work from the 7th century BCE known for its empathetic tone.

title.title: Enuma Elish
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: The Babylonian creation myth describing the rise of the god Marduk and the formation of the world.
title.historical_context: A seminal work of Mesopotamian literature from the 6th century BCE.

title.title: The Sibylline Oracles
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: A collection of prophetic writings attributed to a series of sibyls.
title.historical_context: A composite work from the 2nd century BCE, reflecting a blend of Greek, Roman, and Near Eastern traditions.

title.title: The New Testament: The Gospel of Mark
title.author: Anonymous (Early Christian Tradition)
title.summary: The earliest Gospel in the New Testament, recounting the life and teachings of Jesus.
title.historical_context: A foundational Christian text from the 1st century CE.

title.title: Meditations
title.author: Marcus Aurelius
title.summary: A series of personal writings by the Roman emperor reflecting on Stoic philosophy and self-improvement.
title.historical_context: An enduring work of philosophy from the 2nd century CE.

title.title: The Historia Augusta
title.author: Anonymous
title.summary: A collection of biographies of Roman emperors, blending fact and fiction.
title.historical_context: A controversial historical text from the 3rd century CE.

title.title: The Confessions
title.author: Augustine of Hippo
title.summary: An autobiographical work detailing Augustine's sinful youth and conversion to Christianity.
title.historical_context: A seminal work of Christian philosophy from the 4th century CE.

title.title: The Vulgate Bible
title.author: Jerome (compiler)
title.summary: The Latin translation of the Bible that became the Catholic Church's officially promulgated Latin version.
title.historical_context: An influential text from the 5th century CE.

title.title: The Secret History
title.author: Procopius
title.summary: A controversial account of the inner workings of the Byzantine Empire, written in a caustic tone.
title.historical_context: An insider’s chronicle from the 6th century CE that paints an unflattering picture of the regime.

title.title: The Quran
title.author: Prophet Muhammad (as revealed)
title.summary: The central religious text of Islam, believed to be the word of God as revealed to Prophet Muhammad.
title.historical_context: A foundational text of Islamic faith from the 7th century CE.

title.title: Nihon Shoki
title.author: Multiple Compilers
title.summary: An ancient chronicle of Japanese history, myth, and legend.
title.historical_context: One of Japan's earliest histories, compiled in the 8th century CE.

title.title: Le Morte d'Arthur
title.author: Thomas Malory
title.summary: A retelling of the legends of King Arthur and his knights, combining romance, adventure, and tragedy.
title.historical_context: A seminal work of English literature from the 15th century CE that popularized Arthurian legends.
